test.name: Add ParentChild relationship after proof reaches probable tier
test.description: Per research-schema-spec.md §8, tree.gedcomx.json is updated when a proof reaches `probable` tier or higher. The mid-research-flynn scenario has ps_001 at `probable` and R1 (Thomas → Patrick ParentChild) ALREADY in tree.gedcomx.json. Tests the skill's handling of this expected-state case: confirm the relationship is present and that its source list reflects the supporting assertions.

input.user_message: The parentage proof ps_001 is at `probable` tier. Make sure tree.gedcomx.json reflects the ParentChild relationship between Thomas and Patrick.

judge_context: Should confirm R1 (Thomas I2 → Patrick I1, type ParentChild) already exists in tree.gedcomx.json — it was created when ps_001 reached `probable`, per the proof-conclusion timing rule
Should verify R1's sources list reflects the supporting evidence chain from the underlying proof summary
Should make NO modifications if R1 is already in place and correctly sourced — edit-minimality is the right behavior. If sources are missing, ADD them but don't churn other fields
Should NOT delete R1 even if the proof tier were lowered later — tree edits to remove relationships require explicit re-evaluation, not automatic syncing with proof tier
